feature_name: AEP Library — Custom Adversary Emulation Pathway Builder
design_goal: Give org admins a guided AEP builder that generates a well-structured adversary persona from a plain-language scenario description, lets them refine it in structured fields, and requires them to test it in a live simulator before publishing — making the quality bar clear without requiring prompt engineering expertise.
user_segments: Org admin (primary — creates, edits, publishes custom AEPs)
Campaign manager (secondary — views and selects AEPs; no edit access)
constraints: RBAC: org admins only can create, edit, or delete custom AEPs
Campaign managers can view and use AEPs but see no edit/create affordance
No flowchart or node editor metaphors — AEP branching is emergent LLM behavior, not wired logic
Outcome Criteria fields feed employee risk score calculations — treat as consequential data inputs
Simulator must be a required step before Publish is activated
Draft AEPs should not count toward the custom limit; only Published AEPs count
Version-lock AEPs at campaign creation time — edit must be blocked while referenced in active campaign
Dune-seeded AEPs are globally read-only — no tenant can edit them
selected_strategy: Guided AI-Assist with progressive disclosure (three-step wizard: Define → Configure → Test)
selected_strategy_rationale: Meets admins at their level (describe the scenario in plain language rather than write a system prompt), teaches AEP structure via AI-generated examples, gives advanced users raw prompt access without making it the default, requires simulator testing as a quality gate, and validates the model against KnowBe4 AIDA which uses the same guided-generation pattern.

alternative_strategies.name: Pure free-text prompt editor
alternative_strategies.summary: Admin writes a system prompt directly; structured sections auto-parse or are manually filled
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Too high cognitive load for non-technical admins; produces inconsistent AEP quality; no competitor uses raw prompt as default; risk of unsafe or nonsensical AEPs entering the library

alternative_strategies.name: Fully structured form, no prompt visible
alternative_strategies.summary: Admin fills named fields for each AEP section; system assembles an internal prompt the admin never sees
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Too rigid for novel scenarios; advanced security teams need raw prompt access; inconsistent with 'prompt editor' language in prior strategy doc

tradeoffs: Three-step wizard is slower than a single-page form for experienced admins creating their second or third AEP — Duplicate path mitigates this
AI-generated content may not accurately reflect underlying system prompt behavior if structured fields are cosmetic summaries — must confirm with Eng that fields are authoritative
Simulator as a required step adds friction; admins may do a minimal exchange to unlock Publish rather than genuinely validating behavior
assumptions: Workflow Steps and Branching Logic are AI-generated from the scenario description and editable by admin (not manually authored from scratch)
Outcome Criteria are authored per AEP by the admin in the builder (not globally defined by Dune)
Admin plays the target role manually in the simulator (not AI-vs-AI)
Duplication from Dune-seeded AEPs is a confirmed entry path into the builder
Draft AEPs do not count toward the custom limit — only Published AEPs count
AEP version is locked at campaign creation time
open_issues: [Critical — Both] Are Workflow Steps and Branching Logic AI-generated from the description, or manually authored? Determines whether Configure step starts pre-filled or empty.
[Critical — PM] Are Outcome Criteria defined globally by Dune or authored per AEP? If global, Configure step collapses significantly.
[Critical — Eng] Does admin play target manually in simulator, or is it AI-vs-AI? Determines whether simulator is a chat interface (admin types) or an observation interface (admin watches).
[Medium — Both] Version-locking policy — strategy assumes campaign creation locks AEP version; needs Eng confirmation.
[Medium — PM] Exact custom AEP limit: 4 or 5?
[Medium — PM] Is duplication from Dune-seeded AEPs a confirmed supported path?
risks: Structured fields in Configure step may be decorative if they don't map directly to the system prompt — would erode admin trust in the builder
Simulator as a checkbox: admins do one exchange to unlock Publish without genuine validation — consider minimum exchange count (3 turns) before Publish activates
Outcome Criteria ambiguity: if this is answered as 'globally defined,' the Configure step changes shape and may need a redesign pass
ds_patterns_used: modal (publish confirmation, destructive action confirmation for delete)
badge (Adversary Method, Status: Draft/Published, Simulator: Tested/Not tested)
table (not primary — grid/card view for library)
empty state (Your AEPs section — zero custom AEPs)
CodeEditor or TextArea (system prompt in Advanced mode)
new_patterns_required: Split-pane simulator layout (editor summary left + chat interface right) — requires DS review for responsive handling
Outcome preview strip in simulator (real-time outcome classification indicator) — new pattern, requires DS review
Inline generation loading state (skeleton on structured form fields while AI generates content) — may require DS pattern definition
next_actions: Resolve open issues: confirm Workflow Steps / Branching Logic authoring model with Eng (critical)
Confirm Outcome Criteria scope with PM — per-AEP authored vs. Dune-global (critical)
Confirm simulator interaction model with Eng — admin plays target vs. AI-vs-AI (critical)
Get full Adversary Method taxonomy list from PM (Authority, Urgency, Reciprocity, Curiosity, Scarcity, Familiarity — confirm and finalize)
Audit Stillsuit DS v2 for: wizard step bar, CodeEditor/TextArea, card grid, badge variants, split pane layout, drawer — flag gaps for DS review
Design library grid first (Dune Library + Your AEPs sections + card states)
Design builder wizard third (Define → Configure → Test happy path)
